summary: Iteration 15 — frontend-only verification of (1) MapPage NearbyPanel pill+modal and (2) ARPage compact BallSwitcher. Found one CRITICAL regression in ARPage (caught and fixed): line 383 still referenced <BallSelector> while the import was renamed to BallSwitcher, causing 'BallSelector is not defined' runtime error on every AR page load. After applying the trivial component-name fix, all flows pass on mobile viewport (414x896): camper login → group B01 → first camper, NearbyPanel pill 'Nearby 5' visible, modal opens with 5 spawns in 2-col grid (silhouettes for un-caught with NEW badge, In-Range emerald border, distance in m), close-nearby works, no old standalone ball icon on map. AR launch (?spawn=...) renders ball-switcher-btn 'Pokeball × 278 ⌃', modal lists pokeball=278 (owned), rayball=0 (locked), myrtleball=1 (owned), lunchball=0 (locked); X close, outside-overlay click, and ball selection all dismiss modal. Challenges pill and wallet HUD still render. Zero React/console errors after fix.

ui_bugs.issue: FIXED by tester — ARPage.jsx line 383 still rendered <BallSelector …/> after main agent renamed the import to BallSwitcher. Caused 'ReferenceError: BallSelector is not defined' breaking the entire AR/catch flow. One-token rename to <BallSwitcher (props are API-compatible).

action_items: Tester already applied the trivial fix (BallSelector → BallSwitcher in ARPage.jsx:383). Main agent should review and verify no other dangling BallSelector references remain in repo (BallSelector.jsx still exists at /app/frontend/src/components/BallSelector.jsx — consider deleting or keeping as dead code; lint may flag unused).
Optional cleanup: remove the now-unused /app/frontend/src/components/BallSelector.jsx file if it is no longer imported anywhere.
critical_code_review_comments: BallSwitcher.jsx — clean, accessible (aria-label on trigger, X close), restricts onSelect to owned balls (count>0), handles outside-click & X close. Looks production-ready.

NearbyPanel.jsx — silhouette implementation via `filter: brightness(0) saturate(100%)` is correct and only applied when seenIds set excludes the pokemon id; useEffect on bank fetch is properly cancellable; sorts by haversine distance; 'NEW' badge driven by bank cross-reference. 'X new' badge in pill mirrors that count. Solid.
MapPage.jsx — old standalone ball icon was removed; only NearbyPanel and ChallengesCard render in the top action row. No regression to existing testids (challenges-pill, hud-status).
Repo still contains /app/frontend/src/components/BallSelector.jsx — dead code candidate; safe to delete or leave.

success_rate.frontend: 100% on tested flows after the BallSelector→BallSwitcher fix

test_credentials: Camper: B01 group, first camper (no password). Geolocation mocked via Playwright context to (41.123, -74.456) which the backend snaps to camp center, yielding 5 in-range spawns.
context_for_next_testing_agent: To exercise AR flow you must (a) grant geolocation permission BEFORE goto, (b) login B01 → first camper, (c) dismiss multi-step onboarding by clicking 'Next' a few times, (d) press the bottom 'Catch Pokemon Slot #1!' button to navigate to /ar?spawn=<id>. Otherwise direct /ar route shows 'Returning to map…' panel and BallSwitcher won't render. Ball counts at test time: pokeball=278, myrtleball=1, others=0. Old BallSelector.jsx file still exists; not imported by ARPage anymore.
rca of the issue: Main agent introduced BallSwitcher.jsx and replaced the import in ARPage.jsx (line 11) but left the JSX usage on line 383 referencing the old <BallSelector …/>. Because BallSelector was no longer imported, React threw 'ReferenceError: BallSelector is not defined' the moment a spawn was active and the bottom area tried to render. Reproduction: login as any camper with at least one in-range spawn, tap 'Catch Pokemon Slot #N!' on map → AR page mounts → red error overlay. Fix: rename JSX tag to <BallSwitcher (API-compatible, identical props selected/onSelect/balances/earnProgress).
